title: A Lightweight Embedded PPG-Based Authentication System for Wearable Devices via Hyperdimensional Computing

method.input_representation: Standardized physiological and motion features mapped to high-dimensional hypervectors
method.training_strategy: Adaptive learning process with iterative refinement of the user hypervector

evaluation.context: Attacker Pass Rate achieved at User-Defined Security Level L=3
Calculated as 100% minus Legitimate Pass Rate (97.72%) at Security Level L=1

evaluation_protocol: 10 samples for training and 20 samples for testing per participant; imposters selected from other participants.

reported_limitations: Impact of skin pigmentation/melanin on SNR
Sensor-skin interface gaps in users with small wrist circumferences
Pearson correlation limitation in detecting non-linear dependencies

key_contribution: Integration of PPG and IMU using a lightweight HDC framework
Dimension regeneration mechanism to maximize information density of hypervectors
Adaptive update strategy to mitigate signal drift over time
main_strength: Very low computational and memory overhead (24.5 KB)
High accuracy with minimal training data (10 samples)
Stability across different skin moisture and hair density levels
main_weakness: Lack of active spoofing/presentation attack evaluations
insights.notes_for_review: The system focuses heavily on the hardware-efficiency aspect of HDC, making it a strong example for resource-constrained biometric deployments.
